cards.back: A concise way to create lists in Python using for loops and conditional statements.
A small anonymous function in Python that can take any number of arguments, but can only have one expression.
A function in Python that applies a given function to each item of an iterable (like a list or tuple) and returns a list of the results.
A function in Python that constructs an iterator from elements of an iterable for which a function returns true.
A function in Python that applies a function of two arguments cumulatively to the items of an iterable, from left to right, so as to reduce the iterable to a single output.
